# **LAST WEEK WITH ROB**

Dear Friends,

Welcome back, and thank you for joining me for another edition of “Last Week with Rob.” It’s been a busy week with a return to Albany to start our legislative session. I also had the opportunity to visit one of our district’s gun shows and have been closely following the rising tensions in the Middle East between the U.S. and Iran.

---
|
---
### Action in the Middle East
Earlier this week tensions in the Middle East rose to a high. Thankfully, this tension was deescalated thanks to the steady leadership of President Trump and our exceptional military. Unfortunately, the rash and careless actions of Iran led the senseless murder of innocent lives onboard flight PS752. My thoughts and prayers are with the families of those who lost loved ones due to Iran’s carelessness. Iran must be held accountable and I hope that the international community comes together in condemning the actions of this radical regime. Please continue to pray for the brave men and women of the U.S. military serving in the region and around the world.
|
---
### Dems Vote Against Bail Reforms - Approve Election Interference
Back in Albany, another legislative session got underway. On our first day back, New York’s regressive Democrat senators unanimously voted down an amendment to repeal the disastrous bail reform they enacted last year. Instead, they chose to focus on making our elections less safe and passed automatic voter registration, taking one step closer to allowing illegal aliens the ability to register for voting. This passage of legislation came with little regard for election integrity and included no requirements for voter ID, signatures or other electoral safeguards. New York is a glimpse into what our country can become if AOC and her squad continue to dominate the halls of Congress.
|
---
---
|
### Hamburg Gun Show
Finally, I was able to stop by the Hamburg gun show and meet with many of the firearm enthusiasts from our community. I discussed with vendors and 2A supporters how New York is restricting our second amendment rights and what I am doing to fight back. I also made sure to make a purchase for myself!
---
| As always, feel free to stay up to date on my campaign by following me on Facebook and Twitter at @RobOrttforCongress and let me know what’s going on in your community and your life. Thank you and God Bless.

Rob
Battle-tested Patriot

This is the most critical time for our organizers and volunteers to be talking directly with voters and caucus-goers. And it takes real resources to bring as many people as we can into this movement.

With yesterday's poll showing the race a dead heat, every contribution could make the difference. That's why it's so important we hit our weekly fundraising goals and boost our organizing and advertising efforts week over week through the caucuses.

>
When we launched this campaign just over a year ago, we knew it wouldn't be easy -- the rich and powerful are doing whatever they can to stop our momentum -- but we're not slowing down now.

We're going to keep running this campaign the same way we have since day one -- meeting voters where they are, speaking with them about our plans for big, structural change, and giving them a reason to come out and vote.

And even though the pressure is on, we're going to stick to building our movement the right way: no closed-door only-for-wealthy-donors fundraising events, and no donations from Washington lobbyists, corporate PACs, or PACs of any kind.

The work we need to do right now -- knocking on doors, buying ads, making calls, hosting events, meeting people where they're at and sharing our plans for big, structural change -- is only possible through grassroots donations. And we need another boost if we're going to hit our goal before tonight's deadline.

[**I'm deeply grateful for everything you do as a member of this team, and I wouldn't be asking today if it weren't powerfully important. Can you please chip in before midnight tonight to power this campaign to victory?**](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852bba0/45c30650/1efcd2bc8/66ff6ad9/855171237/VEsEAg/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsImUzc2dkWEpzSUgxOSI6ImV3LWphbjIwZnIiLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6IkVNTjQwRl9FR1JfRVdEX0VfMjAyMDAxMTFfVjFTMV9QIn0%3D/?g=4qwscq_HJ6FcsC0VbCsFfMA "https://secure.elizabethwarren.com/donate/ew-jan20fr?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&refcode=EMN40F_EGR_EWD_E_20200111_V1S1_P")

Thanks for being a part of this,

Elizabeth

And it takes real resources to bring as many people as we can into this movement. | | | "" I'm going to ask if you can make your first donation to our campaign by midnight, but first, I want to talk about why this deadline matters. This is the most critical time for our organizers and volunteers to be talking directly with voters and caucus-goers. And it takes real resources to bring as many people as we can into this movement. With yesterday's poll showing the race a dead heat, every contribution could make the difference. That's why it's so important we hit our weekly fundraising goals and boost our organizing and advertising efforts week over week through the caucuses. "" | If you've saved payment info with ActBlue Express, your donation will process automatically: | | | | "" | "" | | "" | "" | | "" | "" When we launched this campaign just over a year ago, we knew it wouldn't be easy -- the rich and powerful are doing whatever they can to stop our momentum -- but we're not slowing down now. We're going to keep running this campaign the same way we have since day one -- meeting voters where they are, speaking with them about our plans for big, structural change, and giving them a reason to come out and vote. And even though the pressure is on, we're going to stick to building our movement the right way: no closed-door only-for-wealthy-donors fundraising events, and no donations from Washington lobbyists, corporate PACs, or PACs of any kind. The work we need to do right now -- knocking on doors, buying ads, making calls, hosting events, meeting people where they're at and sharing our plans for big, structural change -- is only possible through grassroots donations. And we need another boost if we're going to hit our goal before tonight's deadline. Have a second? I wanted to share the op-ed I wrote for the New York Times on why President Trump was right to take out the terrorist Qassim Suleimani. In service, Tom | [](http://links.action.supportsenatorcotton.com/u/click?_t=62a450578d3d443d9e24ae604e25bd19&_m=4ce164096dd64029a4d7d3da3691baa8&_e=wSQVPD07kzVbaQ35J1txpeIbOVr20HDG7Qxaf9yvAXWRxVHrzHmCvZBWUBmtNNdd7RRh0m9UwqcqDd6d-Q6L51gNIzwdLi4nHG3z71Wv1fXVjazSvmbnHftc95d9Mc1D8vArO4gUENFA8oaSR6j9jyPxAmKWUDD-PZQvqnwYIiOgjfkC2O4F-pa_W9fA_-bl7ExkWS-l6pcVdWGRNYUZm-DoH39hBf8iiRLKj4HS-Vc017iehBKcj4Fej_IVhx3h-u7cswGb8wMYiZsPX-ejNg%3D%3D)

Fellow Conservative,

I wanted to share the op-ed I wrote for the _New York Times_ on why President Trump was right to take out the terrorist Qassim Suleimani.

In service,

Tom

* * *
**The Case for Killing Qassim Suleimani**

_The strike was justified and legally sound._

**By Tom Cotton**
Mr. Cotton is a Republican Senator from Arkansas.

Jan. 10, 2020

Last week, our military and intelligence services brought justice to Qassim Suleimani, Iran's terror mastermind. President Trump ordered General Suleimani's killing after months of attacks on Americans by Iran's proxy forces in Iraq. These attacks culminated in a rocket strike that killed an American and wounded others, then the attempted storming of our embassy in Baghdad. The first attack crossed the red line drawn by the president last summer - that if Iran harmed an American, it would face severe consequences. The president meant what he said, as Mr. Suleimani learned the hard way.

Mr. Suleimani's killing was justified, legal and strategically sound. But the president's critics swarmed as usual. After the embassy attack, a Democratic senator declared that the president had "rendered America impotent." Some Democrats then pivoted after the Suleimani strike, calling him "reckless" and "dangerous." Those are the words of Senator Elizabeth Warren, who also described Mr. Suleimani - the leader of a State Department-designated Foreign Terrorist Organization plotting to kill American troops - as a "senior foreign military official." Senator Bernie Sanders likened America's killing of a terrorist on the battlefield to Vladimir Putin's assassination of Russian political dissidents.

Some Democrats seem to feel a strange regret for the killing of a monster who specialized in killing Americans. The linguist his proxies killed on Dec. 27, Nawres Hamid, was merely his last victim out of more than 600 in Iraq since 2003. His forces have instigated attacks against our troops in Afghanistan. He plotted a (foiled) bombing in Washington, D.C., and attempted attacks on the soil of our European allies. He armed the terrorist group Hezbollah in Lebanon with rockets to pummel the Jewish state of Israel. And he was greeted moments before his death by a terrorist responsible for the bombing of our embassy in Kuwait in 1983.

Some of the president's critics will concede that Mr. Suleimani was an evil man, but many complain his killing was unlawful. Wrong again. He was a United States-designated terrorist commander. As I have been briefed, he was plotting further attacks against Americans at the time of his death. The authority granted to the president under Article II of the Constitution provides ample legal basis for this strike. Furthermore, those who accept the constitutionality of the War Powers Act should recall that Congress's 2001 and 2002 Authorizations for Use of Military Force very much remain in effect and clearly cover the Suleimani operation. This will be a relief to the Obama administration, which ordered hundreds of drone strikes using such a legal rationale.

American forces are in Iraq at the invitation of the Iraqi government, and they have every right and authority to defend themselves. This legal act of self-defense was not only proportionate - it was targeted and brilliantly executed, causing essentially no collateral damage.

So the killing was justified and legally sound. It was also strategically sensible. If Iran's anemic response on Tuesday is any indication, the Suleimani strike has already restored deterrence - and our troops in the region are safer for it. To put it simply, the ayatollahs are once again afraid of the United States because of this bold action, which is forcing them to recalculate their odds. In 2019 alone, Iran's violent provocations included mining ships in the Strait of Hormuz, downing an American drone and threatening the global economy by striking Saudi oil facilities. President Trump chose restraint at the time but promised ferocious retaliation in the event of American casualties. The mullahs must have thought that he was bluffing. Now they're compelled to face the reality of America's vast overmatch of their forces.

The weeks and months ahead will tell whether the Islamic Republic is successfully deterred - but it has been deterred in the past, for example, when Ronald Reagan sank much of the Iranian Navy in 1988. (It has never successfully been appeased, and President Barack Obama's attempts to buy off Iran with his nuclear deal only fueled the regime's imperialism and regional campaign of terror.) Iran is not 10-feet tall. In fact, it's a weak, third-rate power.

Because of this administration's maximum-pressure campaign, the regime manages an economy trapped in a deepening depression. To remain in power, it must mass murder its own people, which it did as recently as November. If maximum pressure is maintained, the ayatollahs will eventually face a choice between fundamentally changing their behavior or suffering economic and social collapse. They may also choose to lash out in a desperate bid to escape this logic, perhaps by making a break for a nuclear bomb. Such impulses must be deterred or, if recklessly pursued, halted with swift and firm action, as the president promised on Wednesday.

This tough-minded approach is not a distraction from America's competition with more serious adversaries like China and Russia, who watch our actions closely in the Gulf for signs of commitment and resolve. Our long-term challenge with China, in particular, directly involves the Middle East's energy resources, to which access remains critical for our allies in the Indo-Pacific, and indeed for China itself - regardless of important strides in America's domestic energy production.

The future of our Iran policy is a critical part of our success in the global competition that will determine the character of this century and the safety of the American republic within it. And recent events have shown we are up to the task.

Tom Cotton ([@sentomcotton](http://links.action.supportsenatorcotton.com/u/click?_t=62a450578d3d443d9e24ae604e25bd19&_m=4ce164096dd64029a4d7d3da3691baa8&_e=wSQVPD07kzVbaQ35J1txpeDtWG_Wi4zvSDyR_7I6eoP6Tx0keVpC1KcJ6TYdxZV9Xz5-BfxoEmGuh3d1IzyuB08FT0I8uht21LEPZA7pc3c6ZFNZS16LILS5M5kUYNYYeC0YSJpKpZksIWPzHNFg5lOKCTpF-RyYhtE5Ojd3sR9s7DdiPxdyEwxrpCgpblFHPYtuYa2I4i5FbBB_Rh45a_mgJIL-W0fSzwN_KMktmKATCODF_960f0d-waLocuyNqX63uXD_bV6DyzsPVNMZKw%3D%3D)) is a Republican Senator from Arkansas.

###

_Senator Cotton was honorably discharged from the U.S. Army.
Images do not imply endorsement by the Department of Defense or any service branch._

| The logic here is pretty simple: The more ads we can run, the more caucus-goers and voters will be introduced to our campaign. And the more people hear about Cory, the more they like him. Hey there, A few days ago we set a goal: **$250,000 to fund our ad program in Iowa.** As of this morning, the team tells me we've hit it! This grassroots community came together and raised _a lot of money_ to get more ads on the air. But there are 22 days left until the caucuses, so why should we stop now? I think we can raise **an additional $300,000** to reach even more caucus-goers and voters. [**Can you help us out? subject: Campaign Newsletter, 1/12/19
body:
| |
---
| | |
---
|
Greetings from the campaign trail!

This past week I stood with my fellow members of Georgia's Jewish community & supporters in response to recent outbreaks of anti-Semitic attacks. Thank you to the American Jewish Committee for your leadership! This event was a great way opportunity to show we will not be intimidated nor deterred in our faith.

---
|
---
|
Next, I met with community leaders and advocates at The Connection in Forsyth County to discuss recovery and addiction services for children and adults. I’ve led on addressing the opioid addiction crisis at the state level, and in Congress, I’ll continue to fight drug addiction in our communities. Recovery is real!

---
|
---
|
Finally, we ended the week by celebrating National Law Enforcement Appreciation Day! Thank you to the brave men and women who put their lives on the line to protect our communities. Your service and sacrifices are appreciated today and every day.

---
|
---
|
Our grassroots campaign relies on the support of Georgians like YOU, and I cannot do this without your help. **I am asking my top supporters to please do the following:**
1. **Donate **[online](https://gmail.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=60170c3a179cc01468053f87a&id=ec7594c140&e=c1dbff800f) – any amount helps!
2. **Like** our [Facebook](https://gmail.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=60170c3a179cc01468053f87a&id=da3b97fa78&e=c1dbff800f) page, and follow me on [Twitter](https://gmail.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=60170c3a179cc01468053f87a&id=41a32cdc44&e=c1dbff800f) and [Instagram](https://gmail.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=60170c3a179cc01468053f87a&id=225e1dba74&e=c1dbff800f).
3. **Share** campaign social media posts online with your friends and family.
4. [**Visit**](https://gmail.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=60170c3a179cc01468053f87a&id=035ee18848&e=c1dbff800f) my website and **[volunteer](https://gmail.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=60170c3a179cc01468053f87a&id=18bda78fb3&e=c1dbff800f)** to make phone calls, canvass your neighborhood, host a meet & greet, or join me for an upcoming event!

---
| Thank you so much for your support in this journey.

Until next week!

-Renee

name: Susan Collins
subject: I stay true to my values, always have and always will
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Collins for Senator
body:
I want to be absolutely clear. I have never wavered from my principles since Day One of serving Maine in the US Senate.
To unsubscribe click [here](http://links.mail1.digitalcampaignstrategies.com/ctt?kn=10&ms=NDE1MTY1NDYS1&r=ODQ4NzI0NzcxNjgxS0&b=0&j=MTY4MDk1MDcyNQS2&mt=1&rt=0)

[](http://links.mail1.digitalcampaignstrategies.com/ctt?kn=6&ms=NDE1MTY1NDYS1&r=ODQ4NzI0NzcxNjgxS0&b=0&j=MTY4MDk1MDcyNQS2&mt=1&rt=0)
_****_
Peter,
The Democratic Senatorial Campaign Committee just launched a new campaign attack claiming that I have "changed."
**Peter, I want to be absolutely clear. I have never wavered from my principles since Day One of serving Maine in the US Senate.**
_I stay true to my values.
I am not swayed by partisan attacks.
I am independent minded.
I am a Mainer through and through._
This is who I am -- **and who I will always be** \-- while serving the great State of Maine.
My opponents aren't running a campaign against my policies or track record; **instead, they are running a campaign based on blatantly false attacks and unfair character assassinations.**
I will make this promise to you: no matter how hard the Left goes after me, no matter how hard they try to bring me down, I will fight back. subject: [bump] This is not the news we wanted to share.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by The Markey Committee
body:
Making sure you saw our fundraising update this week.

Making sure you saw our fundraising update this week.
Joe Kennedy outraised us last quarter, and now we have some ground we need to make up.
**[Can you make a $3 donation right now? We want to make this quarter our best one ever.](https://ma.edmarkey.com/page/m/10c1df9b/693b8b0a/434dc9b6/72de7424/515955466/VEsH/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMDAxMTItZnVsbCJ9/?g=-NfBFVNw52hcsCx35ezxeoA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/markey-january?refcode=em200112-full")**
Thank you for being part of this movement.
\- Team Markey
* * *
#
Friends -
This is not the news we wanted to share. The Boston Globe reported this week that Joe Kennedy outraised us.
When our grassroots team outraised him in the third quarter, we knew he'd put up a tougher fight.
Look, this past quarter was our best ever, but it's clear we have more work to do to make sure we have what we need to compete.
[**Make a $3 donation to our campaign today to help us re-elect Ed. If we 're going to pass the most progressive agenda possible, then we need to keep Ed in the fight. **](https://ma.edmarkey.com/page/m/10c1df9b/693b8b0a/434dc9b6/72de7424/515955466/VEsE/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMDAxMTItZnVsbCJ9/?g=-NfBFVNw52hcsCx35ezxeoA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/markey-january?refcode=em200112-full")
The truth is, Ed does not come from money. He grew up as the son of a milkman and worked his way through college driving an ice cream truck.
Ed has built his career by organizing and supporting grassroots movements capable of doing the hard work on the ground.
That's what we're going to do now, too. We are building a diverse, intergenerational movement of working people who are committed to fighting for our shared progressive values -- like the Green New Deal.
So Ed's counting on you, and he needs you behind him today.
[**Make a $3 donation to our campaign right now to help us continue building our movement.**](https://ma.edmarkey.com/page/m/10c1df9b/693b8b0a/434dc9b6/72de7424/515955466/VEsF/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMDAxMTItZnVsbCJ9/?g=-NfBFVNw52hcsCx35ezxeoA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/markey-january?refcode=em200112-full")
Together, we are going to win.
Let's get it done,
Team Markey
**[DONATE](https://ma.edmarkey.com/page/m/10c1df9b/693b8b0a/434dc9b6/72de7424/515955466/VEsC/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMDAxMTItZnVsbCJ9/?g=-NfBFVNw52hcsCx35ezxeoA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/markey-january?refcode=em200112-full")**

* * *

name: Elizabeth Warren
subject: I might call you!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Warren for President
body:
I'm fighting shoulder-to-shoulder with you to win in Iowa. Make calls to caucus-goers now: | | | [](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852bbfc/45c30650/1ef6d6b67/66ff0712/2804901040/VEsH/?g=4qwscq_HJ6FcsC0VbCsFfMA "https://elizabethwarren.com/call")
---
With the Iowa caucuses in 22 days, and another debate coming up, some candidates would spend this time calling ultra-millionaires and asking them to write big checks. But that's not who I am.

My "call time" is spent talking with grassroots supporters like you -- today, I'm in Iowa, talking with caucus-goers who will shape this race in a few weeks. And if you can pick up the phone to connect with Iowans today, too, I might give YOU a call.

**[You can make calls to caucus-goers right now, from home or anywhere you have an internet connection-- I make a lot of calls to supporters like you from the passenger seat of a rental car when we're traveling.](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852bbfc/45c30650/1ef6d6b67/66ff0712/2804901040/VEsE/?g=4qwscq_HJ6FcsC0VbCsFfMA "https://elizabethwarren.com/call")**

**[All you have to do is click here to get started. And if you get a call from an unknown number, pick up. It might be me!](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852bbfc/45c30650/1ef6d6b67/66ff0712/2804901040/VEsF/?g=4qwscq_HJ6FcsC0VbCsFfMA "https://elizabethwarren.com/call")**

Supporters like you have been crucial in building this movement, and I'm grateful down to my toes that you're in this fight with me. But over the next few weeks, we need to be connecting with caucus-goers and voters to make sure they're in this fight with us, too.

**And if you can help us connect with caucus-goers on the phones today, I want to give you a call to thank you!** It's one of my favorite parts of the day. Because every conversation is a small spark of the democracy we're trying to build together -- one that puts political and economic power into the hands of working people.

**[Can I count on you to make calls to Iowa caucus-goers today? Every conversation we have between now and February 3 helps move the needle, and I couldn't be more grateful. In fact, if you help make calls today, I might give you a call to say thanks!](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852bbfc/45c30650/1ef6d6b67/66ff0712/2804901040/VEsC/?g=4qwscq_HJ6FcsC0VbCsFfMA "https://elizabethwarren.com/call")**

I believe in how we're doing this, and I believe in our power together. We truly make each other stronger.

Thanks for being a part of this,

Elizabeth | **[**MAKE CALLS NOW**](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852bbfc/45c30650/1ef6d6b67/66ff0712/2804901040/VEsD/?g=4qwscq_HJ6FcsC0VbCsFfMA "https://elizabethwarren.com/call")**

name: Senator Jeff Merkley
subject: This is what we did together (wow)
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jeff Merkley for Oregon
body:
| [](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2fc13/6cd27893/537ee82d/1587fd81/1825140595/VEsH/?g=fjbuEq_HJ6FcsCg1a40jRRA "http://www.jeffmerkley.com")
---

| | How do we change the world? Well, a big part of it is what I and other legislators do -- fighting on the "inside" to pass legislation, conduct oversight, and move the levers of power in the government. But that's not enough. It's also about working "outside", with you, to build a movement to support change. After all, democracy is a team sport. We all need to do our part. So, as we enter an election year, I'd like to report to you on what we did together in 2019. In politics, they call it an "off year"--but we certainly didn't take it off. For starters, we made ourselves heard. In 2019 alone, over 400,000 people signed at least one of our petitions calling for change (just over 700,000 times!) Why do these petitions matter? Because they help me make the case in the halls of power. They are a clear demonstration that I'm not alone in calling for reforms, for accountability, for action. And we started to set the stage for taking back the Senate in 2020. As a community, we made critical investments to defend vulnerable Senators Gary Peters, Tina Smith, and Doug Jones--and we helped kick off the campaigns for 2020 Blue Wave champions Sara Gideon, Mark Kelly, and Theresa Greenfield. And perhaps most gratifying of all, to celebrate my birthday, members of our movement sent thousands of dollars to a dozen charitable organizations fighting for the children whom our president has caged near our southern border. Thank you. Like you, I am heartbroken every day about what is being done in our names to these children. As we look ahead to 2020, I know that I'm going to ask a lot of you. The fights ahead are critical, and the outcome is anything but certain. I'm running for re-election, we're fighting to take back the Senate, and--of course--elect a new president! Thank you for your partnership. Thank you for your dedication. And thank you for taking action, again and again and again. Onward! _Jeff Merkley_ Express Donate: If you've stored your info with ActBlue Express, we'll process your contribution instantly: | [$5](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2fc13/6cd27893/537ee82d/1587fdbe/1825140595/VEsE/p/eyJlM3RpZFhSMGIyNWZiMjVsZlgwPSI6IjUifQ%3D%3D/?g=fjbuEq_HJ6FcsCg1a40jRRA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/stand-with-jeff-merkley?express_lane=true&amount=5&refcode=2020-01-12-yearinreview&refcode2=express-buttons") | [$15](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2fc13/6cd27893/537ee82d/1587fdbf/1825140595/VEsF/p/eyJlM3RpZFhSMGIyNWZkSGR2ZlgwPSI6IjE1In0%3D/?g=fjbuEq_HJ6FcsCg1a40jRRA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/stand-with-jeff-merkley?express_lane=true&amount=15&refcode=2020-01-12-yearinreview&refcode2=express-buttons") | [$25](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2fc13/6cd27893/537ee82d/1587fdbc/1825140595/VEsC/p/eyJlM3RpZFhSMGIyNWZkR2h5WldWOWZRPT0iOiIyNSJ9/?g=fjbuEq_HJ6FcsCg1a40jRRA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/stand-with-jeff-merkley?express_lane=true&amount=25&refcode=2020-01-12-yearinreview&refcode2=express-buttons") | [$50](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2fc13/6cd27893/537ee82d/1587fdbd/1825140595/VEsD/p/eyJlM3RpZFhSMGIyNWZabTkxY24xOSI6IjUwIn0%3D/?g=fjbuEq_HJ6FcsCg1a40jRRA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/stand-with-jeff-merkley?express_lane=true&amount=50&refcode=2020-01-12-yearinreview&refcode2=express-buttons")

name: Team Sara
subject: (bump) Bad news.
body:
It's everything people hate about politics.

**Republican dark money group One Nation just launched close to half a million dollars in ads supporting Collins.**
[**Rush a $3 contribution now to help us respond to these ads ->**](https://gideon.bsd.net/page/m/65802e7b/4bef7a87/6ac9ea2e/287f8423/951718097/VEsH/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMDAxMTItMi1iIn0%3D/?g=siAzLVH8x8VdU6OjQM8XC0A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sg-jan-2020?refcode=em200112-2-b")
I have bad news -
**One Nation, a Republican dark money group aligned with Mitch McConnell, launched nearly half a million dollars in ads supporting Susan Collins on Friday.**
Our ability to respond depends on people like you, because the overwhelming majority of the donations we receive come from people giving small amounts. That's why we have to ask:
[**Will you rush a $3 contribution to support Sara Gideon 's campaign and help us respond to these ads?**](https://gideon.bsd.net/page/m/65802e7b/4bef7a87/6ac9ea2e/287f8423/951718097/VEsE/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMDAxMTItMi1iIn0%3D/?g=siAzLVH8x8VdU6OjQM8XC0A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sg-jan-2020?refcode=em200112-2-b")
Look, it's clear these guys are paying Collins back for her support on impeachment. Just this week, she backed McConnell's plan to block all witness testimony during the Senate impeachment trial - and now they're running ads for her?
We're sick and tired of Susan Collins' favor returning brand of politics - which is why we're more committed than ever to electing Sara Gideon, a leader who won't be beholden to Mitch McConnell or special interest groups in Washington.
But we'll need your help to do it, so please, [**chip in now.**](https://gideon.bsd.net/page/m/65802e7b/4bef7a87/6ac9ea2e/287f8423/951718097/VEsF/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMDAxMTItMi1iIn0%3D/?g=siAzLVH8x8VdU6OjQM8XC0A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sg-jan-2020?refcode=em200112-2-b")
\- Team Sara

subject: GOP voter suppression could backfire
disclaimer_text: Paid for by When Democrats Turn Out PAC
body:
|

| | Peter, Florida's GOP legislature attempted to undermine a voter-approved measure restoring voting rights to former felons by imposing an unconstitutional poll tax, but their efforts to keep over a million people from voting could backfire. The four most populous counties in Florida (comprising a third of the state's population) have used a loophole in the law to waive the fees and allow hundreds of thousands to vote in 2020. This could be a game-changer and tip the scales for Democrats. In 2016, millions of Democrats stayed home or were intimidated from voting at all, and Donald Trump won Wisconsin by just 0.77%, Michigan by just 0.23% and Pennsylvania by just 0.72%. They're trying to make this happen again in 2020. That's why we need to know if your voting plans have changed: **As of today, do you plan to vote for Trump in 2020?** [YES](https://turnoutpac.bsd.net/page/m/78bcdfc1/6e9863e8/17df1b4e/1db040a1/3288469966/VEsH/?g=mj_BT60LBwxdW4nnt4whVXA "https://s.surveyplanet.com/jFVqibUR") [NO](https://turnoutpac.bsd.net/page/m/78bcdfc1/6e9863e8/17df1b4e/1db040a1/3288469966/VEsE/?g=mj_BT60LBwxdW4nnt4whVXA "https://s.surveyplanet.com/jFVqibUR") Thank you for your response, WDTO Team [DONATE](https://turnoutpac.bsd.net/page/m/78bcdfc1/6e9863e8/17df1b4e/1db040a7/3288469966/VEsF/?g=mj_BT60LBwxdW4nnt4whVXA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/when-democrats-turn-out-nd?refcode=OUT20200112c.ND&refcode2=voL7WSffChBFJqzXDKHa")

Last we checked, we're slightly off pace for our very first fundraising deadline of 2020. This could spell bad news for our work to protect Minnesota's Senate seat in November.

**Please, rush an urgent contribution of any amount to Tina's campaign before our first deadline of 2020.
